Package org.eventb.core.sc.state

Interface IIdentifierSymbolInfo

All Superinterfaces:
Comparable<ISymbolInfo<?,?>>, IAttributedSymbol, ISymbolInfo<ISCIdentifierElement,IInternalElementType<? extends ISCIdentifierElement>>
All Known Implementing Classes:
IdentifierSymbolInfo, IdentifierSymbolTableTests.TestIdentifierSymbolInfo
public interface IIdentifierSymbolInfo extends ISymbolInfo<ISCIdentifierElement,IInternalElementType<? extends ISCIdentifierElement>>
IIdentifierSymbolInfo is a symbol table symbol info for identifiers, i.e. carrier sets, constants, and variables. It stores the type of an identifier. The name of the identifier is the key of this symbol info.

Clients that need to contribute symbols to the identfier symbol table, IIdentifierSymbolTable, must implement this interface.

Since:
1.0

  • Method Details

    • getType

      Type getType()
      Returns the type of the identifier.
      Returns:
      the type of the identifier

    • setType

      void setType(Type type) throws org.eclipse.core.runtime.CoreException
      Sets the type of the identifier.
      Parameters:
      type - The type to be assigned to the identifier.
      Throws:
      org.eclipse.core.runtime.CoreException - if this symbol is not mutable

    • createSCElement

      ISCIdentifierElement createSCElement(IInternalElement parent, org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor) throws org.eclipse.core.runtime.CoreException
      Create a statically checked identifier element for this symbol with the specified parent.
      Parameters:
      parent - the parent of the element to create
      monitor - a progress monitor, or null if progress reporting is not desired
      Returns:
      the created statically checked identifier element
      Throws:
      org.eclipse.core.runtime.CoreException - if there was a problem creating the element

    • createUntypedErrorMarker

      void createUntypedErrorMarker(IMarkerDisplay markerDisplay) throws org.eclipse.core.runtime.CoreException
      Attaches an appropriate error message to the source element if this identifier could not be typed. This message only makes sense for identifiers that have not been imported.
      Parameters:
      markerDisplay - the marker display to use
      Throws:
      org.eclipse.core.runtime.CoreException - if there was a problem creating the element
